body:
## Problem

`/stats` (Metal Drummer Gear Statistics hub) has full SSR meta + Dataset JSON-LD + FAQPage schema in `api/meta/[...path].js` (line 271) but is **completely absent from `api/sitemap.js`**.

Only `/stats/gear-insights` appears in the sitemap (line 433). The parent hub `/stats` is invisible to Googlebot and AI crawlers unless they discover it via internal links — no sitemap discoverability.

**Verified:** grep `api/sitemap.js` for `'/stats'` returns zero results. grep for `/stats/gear-insights` returns one result (line 433).

This is the same bug class as #1348 (/vs hub) and #1349 (/guides hub) — both fixed.

## Fix

`api/sitemap.js` — add one entry before the `/stats/gear-insights` line:

```js
{ loc: '/stats', priority: '0.9', changefreq: 'weekly' },
{ loc: '/stats/gear-insights', priority: '0.9', changefreq: 'weekly' },  // already there
```

## Impact

- /stats is a Data Authority page (brand-popularity stats for 60+ drummers)
- Dataset schema + FAQPage in SSR — crawlers can see and index it once sitemapped
- Distinct from #1704 (which adds FAQPage to /stats — schema gap, not sitemap gap)

## Verify

1. `curl https://metalforge.io/sitemap.xml | grep '/stats'` — should now return both `/stats` and `/stats/gear-insights`
2. Google Search Console → Sitemaps → confirm /stats appears in indexed URLs within 48h
3. `curl -s https://metalforge.io/api/meta?path=/stats | jq '.articleSchema'` — Dataset schema present ✅

## Done

- [ ] `api/sitemap.js` has `{ loc: '/stats', priority: '0.9', changefreq: 'weekly' }` added
title: SEO: /stats hub — add to sitemap.js (page has full SSR meta but zero crawl discoverability)
